messages.content: What are some very useful, not so know sites like https://12ft.io/ ?

Sites similar to 12ft.io, which is designed to bypass paywalls by taking advantage of the way websites offer unrestricted access to web crawlers, offer a range of services that might include simplifying web pages, providing access to premium content, or helping with research and learning. Here are some lesser-known, but useful websites that you might find interesting:

1. **Outline.com** - Helps users read and annotate news articles with a clutter-free view. Outline strips down articles to the essential content for easier reading. (Note: As of my knowledge cut-off in early 2023, Outline.com may not be operational anymore.)

2. **BugMeNot.com** - Provides shared logins for websites that require you to register in order to view content, thereby saving you time and keeping your email address private.

3. **Sci-Hub** - While controversial and facing legal challenges, Sci-Hub provides free access to millions of research papers that would otherwise be behind paywalls. It's a valuable resource for students and researchers.

4. **Unpaywall.org** - A legal browser extension that finds open access versions of paywalled articles, which helps researchers and students find free scholarly articles.

5. **Library Genesis (LibGen)** - Another controversial source but it's a searchable database of free books and articles on various topics, spanning from novels to academic texts and research papers.

6. **Archive.is** - Takes snapshots of web pages that will not require cookies, JavaScript, or other types of interactive content to view the archived version.

7. **Wayback Machine (Archive.org)** - Allows users to see archived versions of web pages from the past, which can be helpful if a page has been taken down or to observe how a website has changed over time.

8. **PrintFriendly & PDF** - A service that turns any web page into a clean view optimized for printing or saving as a PDF, removing ads and other unnecessary elements.

9. **The Annotated NYT** (**nyt.annotate.tv**) - This site annotates New York Times articles with context, fact checks, and comments.

10. **JustWatch.com** - A streaming search engine that shows you where you can legally watch movies or TV shows online, whether for free or through a subscription service.

11. **InVID Verification Plugin** - A tool for journalists and the general public which allows them to verify the authenticity of videos and images by checking metadata, reverse image searching, and more.

Privacy and ethics should always be considered when using these or similar tools. It's important to respect copyright and data privacy laws, and to be aware that some tools might operate in legal or ethical gray areas. Always ensure that you are in compliance with applicable laws and regulations when using such services.
Those were great! What are some more?

Here are additional resources and lesser-known but useful websites:

1. **Photopea** - An online photo editor that works similar to Adobe Photoshop and can handle a variety of formats, including PSD, XCF, Sketch, and more.

2. **10 Minute Mail** - Provides a disposable email address that expires after 10 minutes, useful for signing up for services you don't want to receive spam from.

3. **FutureMe.org** - Write a letter to your future self and have it sent back to you at a specified date in the future.

4. **Sleepyti.me** - Helps you calculate when to go to bed if you want to wake up refreshed by timing sleep cycles.

5. **Hemingway Editor** - An online writing tool that improves the readability of your text by highlighting dense and complex sentences.

6. **CamelCamelCamel** - A price tracker that shows price history for products on Amazon, helping you determine the best time to make a purchase.

7. **Ninite** - Allows you to download and install multiple programs all at once, saving time on setting up a new computer or updating your current one.

8. **Music Map** - If you're looking for new music, type in the name of an artist you like and Music Map will visually show you other artists with similar styles.

9. **Radio Garden** - Explore live radio by rotating the globe and listen to thousands of live radio stations across the globe.

10. **GTmetrix** - Analyzes the speed and performance of your website, giving you insights and suggestions for improvement.

11. **TinEye** - A reverse image search engine that lets you find where an image came from, how it's being used, if modified versions of the image exist, or if there's a higher resolution version.

12. **Have I Been Pwned** - Check if your email address has been compromised in a data breach. A useful tool for online security awareness.

13. **ManualsLib** - A database of PDF manuals for various products. Handy if you've ever lost a user manual and need to find a replacement.

14. **AccountKiller** - Provides instructions on how to easily delete your account from a wide variety of websites.

15. **Down for Everyone or Just Me** - Allows you to check if a website is down for other people or if the issue is just on your end.

16. **Typing.Com** - A free online tool for improving your typing speed and accuracy.

As with any online service, remember to exercise caution, especially with services that handle sensitive data. Always aim to use tools that prioritize user privacy and data security.
what are some more?

Certainly! Here's a further list of specialized and potentially lesser-known online tools and websites:

1. **DeepL Translator** - A highly accurate translation service considered to be more precise than some other well-known translation tools for many language pairs.

2. **Khan Academy** - Offers free courses on a wide range of subjects, from mathematics to arts and humanities.

3. **Project Gutenberg** - A large collection of over 60,000 free eBooks, particularly useful for students and avid readers of classics.

4. **Privnote** - Send notes that will self-destruct after being read, ensuring your private messages remain confidential.

5. **Internet Archive's Software Collection** - Offers classic PC games, old software, and even retro operating systems which you can experience right from your web browser.

6. **Descript** - A unique audio and video editing tool that allows you to edit multimodal media the way you would edit a text document.

7. **Namechk** - Checks to see if your desired username or vanity URL is still available at dozens of popular social networking and social bookmarking websites.

8. **BuiltWith** - Find out what websites are built with by entering a URL; it’s good for web developers wishing to know the technology stack of competing sites.

9. **E.gg Timer** - A simple countdown timer that can be used for anything from cooking to Pomodoro productivity techniques.

10. **Fake Name Generator** - Generates fake names and identities for use in examples, tutorials, and more. Useful for developers and creatives in need of placeholder data.

11. **Fast.com** - A quick and simple speed test for checking your internet connection, powered by Netflix.

12. **AlternativeTo** - A user-driven service that helps you find better alternatives to the products you love and hate.

13. **WindowSwap** - Lets you view short video clips from other people’s windows around the world, a delightful way to virtually travel and get a glimpse of different places.

14. **Canva** - An easy-to-use graphic design tool with a drag-and-drop format for creating professional designs for various uses.

15. **Cold Turkey** - A productivity program that blocks distracting websites and applications to help you focus on your work.

16. **Charity Navigator** or **GiveWell** - Helps you find and evaluate reputable charities, ensuring your donations are used effectively.

17. **Lumosity** - Brain training app designed to improve memory, increase focus, and feel sharper – using scientifically-verified cognitive games.

Each of these websites provides a unique tool or service that can be useful in various situations, whether for personal productivity, educational purposes, web development, or leisure. Always make sure to use them responsibly, particularly when dealing with tools that handle personal information or content with copyright constraints.
what sites can I look for on BugMeNot?

BugMeNot is a website that provides usernames and passwords to bypass mandatory free registration on websites. However, it is important to note that using someone else's credentials to access a website without permission may violate the terms of service of the website, and in some cases, may be illegal.

Moreover, many websites have measures in place to prevent such shared logins from working, and they might regularly purge accounts that they suspect are being shared via BugMeNot.

Here are the types of sites where you might find shared credentials on BugMeNot:

1. **Forums and Message Boards** - These may require registration to view or post content.
2. **News Websites** - Some may restrict viewing full articles to registered members only.
3. **Download Sites** - Occasionally these require accounts to download files, manuals, or software.
4. **Resource Libraries** - Academic and various professional resources could have a soft paywall.
5. **Software and App Websites** - Registration might be needed to access demos or free versions.

It's worth noting that most high-profile sites and services (like Netflix, Amazon, most email service providers, online banking, etc.) will not be available on BugMeNot, as using shared credentials could lead to serious security risks and legal issues.

Additionally, for ethical and security reasons, it is typically better to create your own account or seek legitimate ways to access content without violating terms of service or compromising personal information. When it comes to paid services or content behind a paywall, consider supporting the content creators by paying for their services.

**Disclaimer:** The information above is provided for educational purposes only. It is essential to adhere to the terms of service of any website and respect digital property and privacy laws.
What are some more websites that are helpful resources that do unquiet things like the paywall ones and the BugMeNot ones?

There are various creative and unique websites out there that offer helpful resources, some serving niche purposes. Here's a list of additional sites providing services that might be considered somewhat off the beaten path:

1. **Every Time Zone** - Offers an easy-to-understand display of different time zones across the globe, useful for coordinating meetings across different locations.

2. **This is Sand** - A unique site where you can create art using different colors of sand. It's a relaxing and creative pastime.

3. **A Soft Murmur** - This service allows you to mix ambient sounds to create a soundscape tailored to your preference, ideal for focus or relaxation.

4. **Eat This Much** - Automatically creates meal plans that meet your diet goals. It can be personalized for any calorie amount and dietary preference.

5. **IFTTT (If This Then That)** - Connects all your services (apps and devices) together so that they work in unison. For instance, you can create an event to automatically save email attachments to Google Drive.

6. **SuperCook** - Finds recipes you can make with the ingredients you already have at home. It’s a smart way to minimize food waste and discover new meal ideas.

7. **What The Font** - If you've ever seen a font on a webpage or an image and wanted to know what it was, upload a screenshot and the site will try to match it with the font.

8. **Zamzar** - A file conversion site that can handle hundreds of file types, converting them to formats that you can use without needing to download dedicated software.

9. **Down Detector** - Provides real-time information about the status of various websites and services, telling you if a particular service is down or experiencing issues.

10. **PDF Escape** - A free, online PDF reader, editor, form filler, and form designer - no downloads or installations required.

11. **OldVersion.com** - Offers older versions of software for people who are not satisfied with the latest updates or whose systems may not support them.

12. **RunPee** - An app that tells you the best times to run to the bathroom during a movie without missing important scenes. It also provides a summary of the first three minutes if you're running late.

13. **Patatap** - An interactive website that lets you create melodies and animations with the touch of a button. It's an audio-visual experience that can be quite mesmerizing.

14. **WebOasis** - A web portal and start page with a collection of links to various resources on the web, from tools to entertainment.

15. **Unsplash** - A great resource for high-resolution photos that you can use for free, contributed by photographers worldwide.

16. **PineTools** - A collection of free online tools for editing images, creating patterns, converting files, and more.

As always, when using online resources, it's important to be mindful of terms of service, your own digital security, and the legality of how you are accessing or using content.
more

Absolutely, there’s a seemingly endless trove of unique websites offering various services. Here are more to explore:

1. **Terms of Service; Didn't Read (tosdr.org)** - Provides a summary of terms of service and privacy policies for many popular websites, giving them ratings for how user-friendly and respectful they are of user rights.

2. **Fakespot (fakespot.com)** - Analyzes reviews for products on Amazon, Yelp, TripAdvisor, and the Apple App Store to estimate how many reviews are likely to be fake or unreliable.

3. **Manuals Online (manualsonline.com)** - A comprehensive collection of user manuals and product guides for a wide variety of electronics, appliances, and consumer goods.

4. **RetailMeNot (retailmenot.com)** - Offers coupons and promo codes for online stores, helping users to find discounts and save money during online shopping.

5. **FlightAware (flightaware.com)** - Provides real-time flight tracking and historical flight data for commercial airline flights, private/GA flights, and airports.

6. **JustDelete.me (backgroundchecks.org/justdeleteme/)** - A directory of links to pages where you can delete your account from various web services.

7. **Scribble Maps (scribblemaps.com)** - Allows you to create custom maps with annotations, shapes, and markers, which can be useful for planning, education, or presentations.

8. **Account Killer (accountkiller.com)** - Provides direct links and instructions on how to delete your accounts from various websites.

9. **MyFridgeFood (myfridgefood.com)** - Tells you what meals you can cook with the food you already have. You check off the ingredients that you have, and it comes up with a list of recipes.

10. **Mathway (mathway.com)** - A math problem solver that can be an invaluable tool for students. It covers a wide range of math topics and can provide step-by-step solutions.

11. **Rainy Mood (rainymood.com)** - An ambient noise generator that simulates the sound of rain. It's meant to be soothing and is often used for relaxation or productivity.

12. **News Map (newsmap.jp)** - Visualize the current news headlines from various publications. The size and color of news stories indicate their popularity and category.

13. **Instructables (instructables.com)** - A community for people who like to make things. Come explore, share, and make your next project with us.

14. **The Rasterbator (rasterbator.net)** - Allows you to create large posters from any image by enlarging the image and dividing it into sections to be printed on multiple pages.

15. **ReviewMeta (reviewmeta.com)** - Analyzes Amazon product reviews and filters out reviews that seem biased or unreliable to give a better sense of what actual customers are saying about a product.

Each of these websites has its particular utility and can be very handy depending on your needs. As with any online tool, it’s important to ensure that you’re comfortable with their privacy policies and how they handle user data before using them extensively.
more

Certainly! The internet houses a wealth of resources with unique tools and services. Here are some more interesting websites:

1. **Radiooooo (radiooooo.com)** - An interactive map that allows you to listen to music from around the world and different decades. It's a musical time-travel experience.

2. **Pixlr (pixlr.com)** - A suite of cloud-based image tools and utilities, including several photo editors that range in complexity, useful for quick edits or in-depth photo manipulation.

3. **Wolfram Alpha (wolframalpha.com)** - A computational knowledge engine that can provide answers to factual queries directly by computing the answer from structured data.

4. **HowLongToBeat (howlongtobeat.com)** - For gamers wondering how much time they might need to finish a game, this website provides average play times, including main storylines, extras, and completions.

5. **LanguageTool (languagetool.org)** - A proofreading service that checks your writing in more than 20 languages for spelling, grammar, and style errors.

6. **Remove.bg (remove.bg)** - Quickly removes backgrounds from images, making it simple to isolate subjects for graphic design or photo manipulation.

7. **CamelCamelCamel (camelcamelcamel.com)** - An Amazon price tracker that provides price drop alerts and price history graphs for products sold by Amazon.

8. **Habitica (habitica.com)** - A free habit-building and productivity app that treats your real-life like a game, with in-game rewards and punishments to motivate you.

9. **Zoho Sheet (sheets.zoho.com)** - An alternative to Google Sheets, offering a powerful spreadsheet application that allows collaboration and is feature-rich.

10. **WhatShouldIReadNext (whatshouldireadnext.com)** - Enter a book you like and the site will suggest what you could read next.

11. **Paper Rater (paperrater.com)** - A free resource that helps improve writing with grammar checks, suggestions, and plagiarism detection.

12. **DaFont (dafont.com)** - Archive of freely downloadable fonts. You can browse by alphabetical listing, style, author, or popularity.

13. **FakeSpot (fakespot.com)** - Helps you identify fake reviews on sites like Amazon, Yelp, and others to ensure you're getting honest consumer feedback.

14. **Coursera (coursera.org)** - Offers online courses from some of the world's best universities. Courses include recorded video lectures, auto-graded and peer-reviewed assignments, and community discussion forums.

15. **Brain.fm (brain.fm)** - Provides music engineered to help you focus, relax, meditate, recharge, and now also includes sleep patterns.

16. **Reelgood (reelgood.com)** - A streaming guide that tells you where you can watch movies and TV shows across a huge number of streaming services.

Keep in mind, the purpose and legitimacy of these sites can vary, so always use discretion and ensure that you're not violating any terms of service when utilizing shared accounts or paywall bypass tools. Remember to also protect your privacy by not sharing personal information on public platforms.
more

Here are additional intriguing websites that offer various unique services or content that might be of interest:

1. **Cool Tools (kk.org/cooltools)** - A website showcasing various gadgets, software, hardware, and books recommended by the DIY community.

2. **CharityWatch (charitywatch.org)** - Provides in-depth analysis and ratings of charitable organizations to help donors make more informed giving decisions.

3. **My 90's TV (my90stv.com)** - A nostalgia-infused way to browse 90's-era TV clips by genre and year, simulating the experience of watching a TV set from that decade.

4. **Honey (joinhoney.com)** - A browser extension that automatically finds and applies coupon codes at checkout for thousands of online stores.

5. **Sparrho (sparrho.com)** - Combines human and artificial intelligence to help research scientists stay up-to-date on the latest research in their field.

6. **EarthCam (earthcam.com)** - Offers live streaming views of landmarks, beaches, and cities from around the world, directly from a network of webcams.

7. **The Moth (themoth.org)** - If you enjoy stories, this site features true, engaging, and often poignant tales told by the people who lived them.

8. **BlindWrite (blindwrite.webflow.io)** - A unique online tool that darkens your screen and allows you to write "blind," enabling you to write more freely and without self-censorship.

9. **Radiooooo (radiooooo.com)** - Gives users the ability to select a country and a decade so they can listen to popular music from that time and place.

10. **Tineye (tineye.com)** - This reverse image search engine allows you to see where an image came from, how it's used, if modified versions exist or locate high resolution versions.

11. **Hackaday (hackaday.com)** - A blog that publishes posts about hardware and software hacks and shares these projects often with detailed instructions.

12. **Hacker News (news.ycombinator.com)** - A social news website focusing on computer science and entrepreneurship, run by the startup incubator Y Combinator.

13. **Lean Domain Search (leandomainsearch.com)** - Helps you find a quality domain name by pairing your search phrase with other keywords commonly found in domain names.

14. **Nomad List (nomadlist.com)** - Provides detailed information about various cities around the world, including cost of living, weather, internet speed, and other metrics for digital nomads.

15. **Open Culture (openculture.com)** - A resource that features free cultural & educational media. Offers free courses, audiobooks, textbooks, language lessons, movies, and more.

16. **Downsub (downsub.com)** - Generate and download subtitles for YouTube, VLive, Viki, VIU, and more. Useful for videos that don't provide subtitles or where you want to analyze text.

Please note that while these sites offer unique tools and resources, you should always exercise discretion and diligence with regards to the content you access or download, especially regarding your privacy and cybersecurity.
Great! What are some more really cool and unique ones like my90stv.com ?

If you are intrigued by the unique experience provided by sites like my90stv.com, which allows you to immerse in the nostalgia of past media, here are more cool and specialized websites with distinct concepts:

1. **My 80's TV** (my80stv.com) - Enjoy a similar experience to My 90's TV, but with a focus on 1980's content, including cartoons, commercials, and music videos.

2. **Drive & Listen** (driveandlisten.herokuapp.com) - Virtually drive through cities around the world while listening to local radio stations, simulating the experience of driving in a new place.

3. **Internet Arcade** (archive.org/details/internetarcade) - The Internet Archive offers hundreds of arcade games from the 1970s through to the 1990s, playable in a web browser for free.

4. **Window Swap** (window-swap.com) - View short video clips from someone else’s window from various locations around the world, offering glimpses into the view they see every day.

5. **Radio Garden** (radio.garden) - Explore live radio by rotating a 3D globe, and listen to radio stations live from all over the world.

6. **National Geographic's MapMaker Interactive** (mapmaker.nationalgeographic.org) - Customize your own maps using an array of themes and tools, perfect for educational purposes or personal interest in geography.

7. **Scream Into The Void** (screamintothevoid.com) - Type your frustrations or secrets into the text box, hit 'Scream' and your text will be irreversibly scrambled – it’s therapeutic digital catharsis.

8. **Pixel Thoughts** (pixelthoughts.co) - A 60-second meditation tool to help clear your mind, set against the backdrop of a starry sky.

9. **The Useless Web** (theuselessweb.com) - Click a button and be taken to one of many entertaining, strange, or just plain silly websites that have no other purpose than whimsy or distraction.

10. **A Good Movie to Watch** (agoodmovietowatch.com) - Suggestions for movies to watch based on a specific mood, on different streaming platforms, with an emphasis on lesser-known titles.

11. **Bored Button** (boredbutton.com) - Hit the button to be taken to randomly entertaining interactive websites, games, and quizzes.

12. **City Guesser** (virtualvacation.us/guess) - A game that drops you into a random city via video and you guess your location based only on visual clues.

13. **Living Room Concerts** (broadwayworld.com/livingroomconcerts) - Watch performances by Broadway and theater stars from the comfort of your own living room.

14. **Plague Simulator** (plague.com) - Learn how diseases spread by simulating and adjusting different parameters, watching the impact in real-time on a world map.

15. **Strobe.cool** - An intense visual experience that can only be described as a digital 'strobe light'. Be cautious if you're sensitive to flashing lights.

16. **Reel Doozy** (reeldoozy.com) - Offers an interactive way to create short, looping videos with a 3D effect, playing on the trend of GIFs and boomerangs.

As with all online resources and experiences, it's important to engage with them responsibly, especially when navigating to random sites or handling visually intense experiences.
what are some more really cool and unique websites that are not so known?

Discovering cool and unique websites can be exciting, especially when they offer services, entertainment, or information that’s out of the ordinary. Here are several more that might interest you:

1. **Akinator (akinator.com)** - A web genie that can guess any real or fictional character you're thinking of by asking you a series of questions.

2. **Little Alchemy 2 (littlealchemy2.com)** - A simple but addictive game where you combine different elements to create new ones, with the goal of discovering all possible combinations.

3. **Ptable (ptable.com)** - An interactive periodic table of elements with dynamic layouts showing names, electrons, oxidation, trend visualization, and more.

4. **CALM (calm.com)** - Though the full service has a fee, CALM offers guided meditations, stories, and music designed to help reduce anxiety and improve sleep.

5. **Geoguessr (geoguessr.com)** - A geography game that drops you onto a random spot on Google Street View and you must guess your location based only on your surroundings.

6. **This Person Does Not Exist (thispersondoesnotexist.com)** - Shows you images of people who do not actually exist, generated by artificial intelligence.

7. **The True Size Of... (thetruesize.com)** - Explore the true size of countries and compare them by dragging them around a Mercator projection map.

8. **This X Does Not Exist (thisxdoesnotexist.com)** - A curated list of websites that provide computer-generated images of different things like cats, articles, startups, etc., all created by generative adversarial networks (GANs).

9. **Two Foods (twofoods.com)** - Compare the nutritional information between two foods in a simple and visual way.

10. **Incredibox (incredibox.com)** - A music-making site that lets you create your own music by managing a band of beatboxers.

11. **Patook (patook.com)** - A platonic friends-only matching service that connects you with people nearby who share common interests.

12. **The Pattern Library (thepatternlibrary.com)** - Free-to-use patterns for your projects by amazing designers from around the world.

13. **MyNoise (mynoise.net)** - An extensive collection of soundscapes and white noise generators that can be used for focusing, relaxing, or sleeping.

14. **Future Me (futureme.org)** - Write a letter to your future self and it will be emailed back to you at a specified date.

15. **Crossfade.io (crossfade.io)** - A simple web app that enables you to blend and mash-up audio tracks for fun or mixtape purposes.

16. **Cleverbot (cleverbot.com)** - Engage in a conversation with an AI that uses previous interactions to learn how to better respond to inputs.

These websites range from educational and informative to just plain fun. They're examples of the diversity of the web’s offerings beyond the usual well-known sites. As with the use of any online service, practice good online habits regarding privacy and safety.
